gitignore_audit.verdict: all gitignore n6/ rules target the legacy shared/n6/ subtree (now decommissioned); current top-level n6/ + atlas.append.*.n6 are NOT covered. Backlog is purely a manual-commit-deferral, not a silent-exclusion bug.

pattern_diagnosis.last_n6_commit_msg: fix(kick): live-spawn shell-quote bug — route prompt via --task-file (multibyte safe)
pattern_diagnosis.tracked_kind_pattern: exclusively rollup/historical/batch/kick shards (e.g., kick-architecture, anima-historical-from-nexus-2026-04-26, cross-engine-meta-roadmap-2026-04-26, m3-deeper-2026-04-26, r4-deeper-2026-04-26) — these were committed via deliberate batch commits like 'feat(53-batch-3): 5 atlas append shards 생성 → 162/162 ALL CLEAN'
pattern_diagnosis.untracked_kind_pattern: all 77 untracked are PER-CYCLE ω-cycle witness shards emitted by tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa (deterministic emit). Each was generated when an emitter wrote a witness JSON + invoked --absorb (or sweeper picked it up). The ingest tool writes to disk + leaves git-staging to the operator (correct per hooks=NONE policy).
pattern_diagnosis.root_cause: Manual-commit-deferral. The omega_cycle_atlas_ingest tool emits shards correctly (own:atlas-absorb-mandatory satisfied — lint PASS total=83 orphans=0 already=83) but commits are a separate human/CI step. During the prior session's high-cadence ω-cycle work (47 hexa_sim + 13 meta_engine + 9 roadmap_engine + 4 cross_engine + 3 kick + 1 selftest residue all generated 2026-04-25 → 2026-04-26), commits batched the rollup shards but skipped per-cycle shards. Net: 77 valid shards on disk, all absorbed-by-lint-criterion, but git-untracked.
pattern_diagnosis.policy_compliance: raw 77 (audit-append-only-ledger) is LOCALLY satisfied — shards are append-only files, never mutated. But raw 77 in spirit + own:atlas-absorb-mandatory triad goal demand they be tracked in git so the audit ledger survives repo clones / cross-host execution / launchd reboot. Untracked = local-only ledger = breach of the cross-host audit guarantee.
pattern_diagnosis.no_silent_exclusion: git check-ignore -v on samples returns exit 1 (no rule matched). Backlog is not a gitignore bug; it is purely the human/cron commit step never having been run for these shards.

strategy_options.name: A. One-shot bulk add+commit
strategy_options.command_sketch: git add n6/atlas.append.*.n6 && git commit -m 'chore(atlas): track 77 ω-cycle witness shards (backlog absorption)'
pros: atomic — either all 77 land or none
cons: reviewer hostile — 77 shards / 6202 lines / 387KB collapsed into 1 commit
history loss — cannot bisect to find which shard introduced X
raw 77 spirit weakened — append-only-ledger commits should be granular per emission event, not coalesced

strategy_options.name: B. Per-cycle commit (one commit per shard)
strategy_options.command_sketch: for slug in <77 slugs>; do git add n6/atlas.append.$slug.n6 && git commit -m "feat(atlas): absorb $slug ω-cycle witness shard (parent: <witness JSON path>)"; done
pros: each commit references parent witness JSON for full provenance
raw 77 spirit fully honored — 1 commit per emission event
cons: 77 commits → high history noise; obscures unrelated work
if the chain breaks mid-loop, partial state requires recovery
diverges far from origin/main (already +12 commits → would become +89)

falsifier_verdicts.F40_strategy_tier1: PARTIAL — granularity overshoots; reviewer drowns in noise
falsifier_verdicts.F43_cron_collision: RISK — 77 separate add+commit chains, collision window per chain

strategy_options.name: C. Coalesce by engine cluster (5 engine commits + 1 orphan-disposition commit = 6 total)
strategy_options.command_sketch: (see proposed_command_sequence below — 6 single-Bash-chain `git add … && git commit -m …` invocations, one per cluster)
pros: balanced granularity — preserves the 5-engine taxonomy already used in design/<engine>/ subtree (hexa_sim/meta_engine/roadmap_engine/cross_engine/kick)
reviewer-friendly — 6 commits with semantic cluster names
each commit message can reference the cluster + sample witnesses for provenance traceability
matches the precedent set by tracked rollup shards (cross-engine-meta-roadmap-2026-04-26, m3-deeper-2026-04-26, r4-deeper-2026-04-26 — committed as cluster rollups)
raw 77 honored — append-only-ledger granularity at engine-cluster level (the meaningful unit, since witnesses are organized by engine)
F43 cron-collision-safety — each cluster is a single-Bash-chain `add && commit`
cons: loses per-shard bisect — within a cluster, cannot bisect to single shard
requires cluster-name discipline in commit messages

strategy_options.name: D. Defer + add to .gitignore (give-up option)
strategy_options.command_sketch: echo 'n6/atlas.append.*.n6' >> .gitignore && git add .gitignore && git commit -m 'chore(gitignore): exclude per-cycle atlas shards as local-only'
cons: VIOLATES raw 77 (audit-append-only-ledger) — ledger must be cross-host accessible, not local-only
VIOLATES own:atlas-absorb-mandatory spirit — triad goal is durable absorption, not local-only stash
VIOLATES F30 retroactive-clean precedent set by atlas-absorb-triad cycle (which absorbed 81 witnesses; gitignore-exclusion would un-do that work)
future ω-cycles' shards become silently excluded — breaks lint's absorption-via-shard-existence criterion when the shard is on machine A but reviewer is on machine B

tier_1_strategy.rationale: Strategy C is the unique Pareto-optimal point: it (1) honors raw 77 append-only-ledger spirit at the meaningful granularity level (engine = the design/ subtree taxonomy already used), (2) is reviewer-friendly (6 commits, not 77 or 1), (3) matches precedent (tracked rollup shards already use cluster-rollup commit pattern — 'feat(atlas-cross-repo): n6-architecture historical absorption shard' etc.), (4) satisfies F43 cron-collision-safety (each cluster commit is single-Bash-chain), (5) preserves provenance via commit messages referencing the engine subtree and sample witness JSONs. A is reviewer-hostile, B is granularity overshoot, D is policy violation.
tier_1_strategy.promotion_status: TIER-1 promoted; A=Tier-2 fallback (acceptable if user prefers speed over reviewability); B=Tier-3 (over-engineered); D=REJECTED (raw 77 violation)

atlas_n6_modification_check.interpretation: atlas.n6 was NOT mutated during the 77-shard backlog accumulation. The current emission flow uses shard-only mode (one shard file per cycle) without delta-merging into main atlas.n6. This is consistent with raw 65 idempotence (re-running ingest is byte-identity-safe) and raw 77 (each shard is its own append-only file). atlas.n6 main growth happens via separate batch promotion commits (e.g., commit c5ea4e33 'feat(53-batch-3): 5 atlas append shards 생성 → 162/162 ALL CLEAN'). No atlas.n6 commit needed in the proposed sequence.
